name: Ossuary Compact
identity:
The Ossuary Compact is a covenant older than the Reach itself, bound together not by loyalty or territory but by the shared practice of bone-ink necromancy. Its members — Inkbloods, Bonewardens, and the rare independent practitioners — believe that death is not an ending but a transaction. The dead owe debts. The living inherit obligations. And the proper application of ink, bone, and will can rewrite the terms of both.

The Compact operates through a network of ossuaries, scriptoriums, and black-market bone dealers scattered across the frozen wastes. Their primary trade is in written contracts with the dead: binding a restless spirit to service, trading years of unlife for a single favor, or negotiating passage through territory claimed by ancient grave-pacts. These contracts are written in blood-ink on flayed skin or carved into bone tablets, and breaking them carries consequences that make death look merciful.

To outsiders, the Compact is a macabre curiosity at best and a mortal threat at worst. Their methods are undeniably effective — a properly negotiated compact can turn a hostile wraith into an ally, seal a bloom-infected district against further spread, or grant temporary access to the memories of the long-dead. But the price is always more than it first appears, and the Compact's scribes are not above obscuring the fine print.

missions.title: Bone Harvest
missions.description: Recover intact bone samples from a bloom-corrupted graveyard. The Compact's ink requires specific mineral compositions.

missions.title: Inscribe the Wards
missions.description: Carry blood-ink ward stones to three settlements on the frontier. Protect them from bloom-spawn long enough for the ink to set.

missions.title: Broken Compact
missions.description: A rogue Bonewarden has broken their compact and fled with a cache of signed bone tablets. Recover the tablets — the Bonewarden is optional.

missions.title: The Dead Negotiate
missions.description: An ancient grave-pact has been triggered by Engine destabilization. Negotiate with the awakened dead to prevent a massacre — or forge a new compact.
